title: For Loops
description: Use range() and for loops to repeat code a known number of times.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now Activity

Answer these 10 questions from Selection Part 2 before starting loops.

Not sure, help me?

Selection runs different code depending on a condition. Use if condition:, optional elif condition:, and else: as the fallback. Conditions use ==, !=, <, >, <=, >=, and and or. The controlled code is indented.

steps.title: The Range Function
steps.content:
The Range Function

range() creates a sequence of numbers for a loop to use.

range(5)        # 0, 1, 2, 3, 4
range(1, 6)     # 1, 2, 3, 4, 5
range(0, 10, 2) # 0, 2, 4, 6, 8

The start is included. The stop is not included. The third number is the step size.

Click run to see what happens
steps.js: if (window.PyLearn && PyLearn.createEditor) PyLearn.createEditor({ containerId: "for-range-demo", initialCode: "for i in range(1, 6):\n    print(i)", persist: false });

steps.title: Step Through Range
steps.content:
Step Through Range

Click through each stage to see how Python decides which numbers are produced.

steps.title: Range Practice
steps.content:
Range Practice

Practise reading and writing range() calls.

steps.title: Writing a For Loop
steps.content:
Writing a For Loop

A for loop repeats code once for each value in a sequence.

for i in range(5):
    print(i)

i is the loop variable. The indented code is the code that repeats.

Click run to see what happens
steps.js: if (window.PyLearn && PyLearn.createEditor) PyLearn.createEditor({ containerId: "for-line-demo", initialCode: "for i in range(5):\n    print(i)", persist: false });

steps.title: For Loop First Lines
steps.content:
For Loop First Lines

Write the first line only. Remember the colon.

steps.title: For Loops With Print
steps.content:
For Loops With Print

To print each number, put print(i) on the next line and indent it.

for i in range(1, 4):
    print(i)

Click run to see what happens
steps.js: if (window.PyLearn && PyLearn.createEditor) PyLearn.createEditor({ containerId: "for-print-demo", initialCode: "for i in range(1, 4):\n    print(i)", persist: false });

steps.title: For Loops With Print Practice
steps.content:
For Loops With Print Practice

Write complete loops. Use a new indented line for the code inside the loop.

steps.title: End of Lesson Exam
steps.content:
End of Lesson Exam

Complete the exam to check your understanding of range and for loops.

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless Practice

Answer any 5 questions correctly to complete endless practice.

quizQuestions.q: Write a range() call that produces 5, 6, 7, 8, 9.
Write the first line of a for loop where i goes from 1 to 10. Include the colon.
Write a complete for loop that prints the numbers 1 to 5 on separate lines.
Write a complete for loop that prints the even numbers 0, 2, 4, 6 and 8 on separate lines.
Which first line repeats exactly 3 times?
Write a complete for loop that prints 10 to 15 inclusive.

options: The colon adds one extra number
quizQuestions.q: Why does range(1, 6) print 1 to 5 when used in a loop?
